title: Pale Offal
excerpt: Offal eaters are a group all on their own. You either love offal or you just can't resist it. Peter Veldsman wanted pale offal, and so we made it for him. I got to teach him a thing or two about offal as well. He took all the leftover offal home with him.
html: Offal eaters are a group all on their own.  You either love offal or you just can't resist it.  Peter Veldsman wanted pale offal, and so we made it for him.  I got to teach him a thing or two about offal as well.  He took all the leftover offal home with him.

recipe.title: Pale Offal
recipe.author: Marinda Engelbrecht

items: 1 clean offal with trotters, tripe etc. (Delico's is in my opinion the cleanest and best)

steps: Cook the offal, including the pork trotters, water, vinegar, onion, black pepper, cloves, nutmeg, bay leaves, white pepper, ground coriander and sugar, for 2 to 3 hours. (Keep an eye on the water level. Not too much water, this isn't soup. The offal will start to become tender.)
Add the mutton neck and cook for another 1 hour.
Add the onions and potatoes and cook until soft and the meat starts falling off the bones.
Taste and adjust to your liking if needed.

notes: It's always tasty when the offal is left to stand for a while until it turns nice and sticky.
